@ysytenkoff @ianbremmer Some studies for states with higher poverty rates and larger rural populations show vote by mail drives more significant turnout increases, but more data is needed. So, there are admittedly data gaps. Studies show no significant increase in fraud. https://t.co/tQZSQng7Pc

@ysytenkoff @ianbremmer And here is a link to a study by Stanford University that uses data from the three states that have had vote by mail the longest. It basically shows no change in party participation rates and a modest increase in overall turnout. https://t.co/GOuAQlztSl

@ysytenkoff @ianbremmer 2/2 The two work together to improve the system. The former has its biggest impact in primaries (where turnouts are lowest) and the latter in general elections. An individual may not like where the mean goes, but higher turnout yields a more representative mean.

@ysytenkoff @ianbremmer Rank Choice Voting limits extreme outcomes, especially in primaries, by capturing true voter preferences and is distrusted by the far left. Mail in Voting limits extreme outcomes by increasing participation, moving outcomes to the true mean, and is distrusted by the far right.
@elonmusk Then endorse solutions and not parties. That’s what independent thinkers do. They fill the idea gap. They don’t jump on the train of bad ideas (no matter which party is driving the train).

Exactly. The way to do that is not by endorsing Republicans, but by supporting reforms like rank choice voting and broad access to vote by mail. To be clear, things like beef and concrete are contributors - they’re just not “huge”. You have to follow all the links for how things are made and used in an electric economy, and - when you do - you find the carbon emitted to move electrons is the biggest contributor by far.

I love this quote: “low-carbon energy systems would solve about three-fourths of the climate change challenge”. I’m shocked when people say “beef” or “concrete” are “huge”contributors. The chain always leads to energy production dwarfing everything else. https://t.co/FP93LYNAcw
